acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] serde roundtrip tests for new wire types.
- [ ] TxnDeltaV1 rejects duplicate op keys and preserves canonical ordering.
- [ ] Conversions to and from core domain types are covered for at least one bead and note case.
description:
**Problem**
Realtime events need a canonical delta representation that is not tied to the legacy git wire format. Today only src/git/wire.rs exists, so the realtime lane has no typed delta basis for EventBody and no canonical op ordering.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.7 (delta schema choice) and 2.4 (TxnDeltaV1 fields)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (WireBeadPatch, NotesPatch, TxnOpKey, TxnDeltaV1)

**Files:** src/core/wire_bead.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/git/wire.rs (reference only)
design:
**Design**
- Define core wire structs: WireBeadPatch, WireNoteV1, NotesPatch (Omitted vs AtLeast), NoteAppendV1, and TxnOpV1.
- Define TxnOpKey and represent TxnDeltaV1 as a canonical map keyed by TxnOpKey to enforce unique ops and stable ordering for hashing and encoding.
- Keep field names compatible with legacy wire to ease conversion, but keep types in core to avoid coupling realtime to git.
- Provide conversions between domain types (Bead, Note, Tombstone, DepEdge) and wire structs when lossless.
- Document notes rule: patches omit notes by default; if present they mean set union only (no truncation).
title: Phase 2: Core wire delta types

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] TxnDeltaV1 roundtrips with new ops
- [ ] Legacy dep ops removed
- [ ] TxnOpKey includes dot for add ops

**Invariants to re-verify**
- [ ] TxnOpKey uniqueness preserved for multiple dots on same value
- [ ] No labels/notes on WireBeadPatch; no dep upsert/delete ops
description:
**Problem**
WireBeadPatch carries labels and NotesPatch; deps are LWW ops. We need explicit OR-Set ops.

**Files**
- src/core/wire_bead.rs
design:
Remove:
- WireBeadPatch.labels
- NotesPatch from patch
- WireDepV1/WireDepDeleteV1
Add:
- WireDotV1, WireDvvV1
- WireLabelAddV1/RemoveV1
- WireDepAddV1/RemoveV1
Update TxnOpV1 and TxnOpKey (include dot in add keys).
title: Wire ops: Label/Dep add/remove + dot/dvv; remove labels/notes from patch

description:
## What's Wrong
`Request::Init` ignores errors from `ensure_repo_loaded` (TODO in code). Also `SyncWait` behavior is duplicated: `Daemon::handle_request` returns Synced immediately, but `run_state_loop` has special-case barrier logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/core.rs:585 (TODO: ignores ensure_repo_loaded errors after init)
- src/daemon/core.rs:559 (SyncWait returns immediately)
- src/daemon/server.rs:45 (SyncWait handled as a barrier before calling daemon)

## Why It Matters
Init can report success even if repo state isn't loadable, leading to confusing follow-on failures. Duplicated SyncWait logic makes correctness brittle and creates drift.

## Suggested Fix
- Make Init return an error (or include warning details) if `ensure_repo_loaded` fails.
- Consolidate SyncWait into one place (prefer state loop barrier) and remove/clarify the other path.

## Acceptance
- `bd init` fails (or returns structured warning) when it cannot load the repo state.
- `SyncWait` has a single source of truth (no duplicate paths).
title: Daemon init should not ignore ensure_repo_loaded errors; dedupe SyncWait

description:
**Problem**
A strict canonical v1 store with complete files/checksums but no `last_write_stamp` currently detects as already migrated, yet `bd migrate to 1` can still rewrite it. The migration snapshot serializer synthesizes `last_write_stamp` from state data even when the parent meta had no stamp, so byte-for-byte tree comparison fails and the executor mints a needless commit.

That violates the hard-cutover metadata policy to preserve `last_write_stamp` if present and otherwise keep it absent, and it breaks the no-op contract for already-canonical stores.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md

**Design**
Keep migration metadata preservation exact.

1. Stop synthesizing `last_write_stamp` during migration snapshot serialization when the source meta had none.
2. Preserve the existing max-of-present-parent-stamps behavior only when at least one side already carried a meta stamp.
3. Add a regression test for a strict v1 store with checksums and no `last_write_stamp` proving detect says current and execute remains a true no-op.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Canonical v1 stores with no `last_write_stamp` stay true no-ops under `bd migrate to 1`.
- [ ] Migration preserves absence of `last_write_stamp` when the source meta had none.
- [ ] Tests cover the no-stamp canonical case directly.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 4cd04383: migration snapshot serialization now preserves an absent meta.last_write_stamp instead of synthesizing one from state. Non-empty canonical v1 stores without a last_write_stamp remain true no-ops, and migration coverage now pins that behavior. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration follow-up: canonical no-op must preserve absent last_write_stamp

acceptance_criteria: - [ ] git commands used in RealtimeFixture fail fast with clear error messages.\n- [ ] No silent ignore of non-zero exit status.
description:
**Problem**
- tests/integration/fixtures/realtime.rs runs git commands via Command::output() but ignores exit status.
- If git fails (missing binary, permission), tests fail later with confusing errors.

**Files**
- tests/integration/fixtures/realtime.rs
design: Create a small helper (e.g., run_git(args, cwd) -> Result<()>) that checks status.success(), includes stderr/stdout in error, and is used for init/config/remote commands. Consider returning Result from init_git_repo and wiring RealtimeFixture::new to propagate the error (or panic with context).
title: Fixtures: validate git init in RealtimeFixture

description:
**Problem**
beads-rs lacks the Convoy issue type - a cross-project tracking primitive with reactive completion. Convoys track multiple issues (potentially across repos) and automatically close when all tracked items complete. This enables coordinated multi-project work.

**What Convoys Do (from Go beads)**
Convoy is a lightweight tracking container that uses the 'tracks' dependency relation (non-blocking). When any tracked issue closes, convoy checks if all tracked items are complete and auto-closes if so. This is reactive completion - no polling needed.

Use cases:
- Track related work across multiple repos
- Group cross-cutting concerns (security audit, docs update, etc.)
- Coordinate releases (track all pre-release tasks)
- Monitor distributed swarm work

**Go Implementation**
Type definition in internal/types/types.go:
- IssueType = TypeConvoy (line 493)
- Uses standard Issue fields (no custom fields)
- Relies on 'tracks' dependency relation (DependencyType)

Dependency tracking (internal/types/types.go line 713):
- DepTracks: convoy.issue_id tracks target.depends_on_id
- Non-blocking (doesn't affect ready work calculation)
- Direction: convoy → tracked issue

Reactive completion (internal/storage/sqlite/transaction.go lines 583-650):
When any issue closes, query for convoys tracking it:
```sql
SELECT i.id FROM issues i
JOIN dependencies d ON d.issue_id = i.id AND d.type = 'tracks'
JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id AND l.label = 'gt:convoy'
WHERE d.depends_on_id = ? AND i.status != 'closed'
```
For each convoy, check if ALL tracked items are closed. If yes, auto-close convoy.

Note: Uses 'gt:convoy' label instead of issue_type for Gas Town separation.

**Design for Rust**
Data model (src/core/):
No special fields needed - convoys are regular beads with:
- Standard Issue fields
- 'tracks' dependencies to tracked items
- Reactive completion logic in close handler

Reactive completion (src/daemon/ or src/core/):
When closing any bead:
1. Find all convoys that track this bead (traverse 'tracks' edges incoming)
2. For each convoy, check if all tracked items are closed
3. If yes, auto-close convoy with reason "All tracked items complete"

CLI (src/cli/):
- bd create --type convoy "Track release work"
- bd dep add <convoy-id> <tracked-id> --type tracks
- bd dep list <convoy-id> -t tracks (show what convoy tracks)
- bd list --type convoy (filter convoys)

**Design Notes**
- Convoy is more about behavior (reactive close) than structure
- 'tracks' relation must be non-blocking (doesn't affect bd ready)
- Multi-repo tracking needs cross-repo dependency support
- Auto-close should include metadata (which issues triggered completion)
- Consider: Should convoy completion cascade to parent convoys?

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Convoy type constant defined
- [ ] 'tracks' dependency type added to DependencyType enum
- [ ] DepTracks marked as non-blocking (doesn't affect ready work)
- [ ] Bead close handler checks for tracking convoys
- [ ] Convoy auto-closes when all tracked items are closed
- [ ] bd create --type convoy works
- [ ] bd dep add supports --type tracks
- [ ] bd dep list shows tracks relationships
- [ ] Tests for convoy reactive completion
- [ ] Tests for convoy with cross-repo tracked items (if multi-repo supported)
- [ ] Migration from Go beads preserves convoy type and tracks relations

**Files to study in Go beads:**
- tmp/beads/internal/types/types.go (line 493: TypeConvoy, line 713: DepTracks)
- tmp/beads/internal/storage/sqlite/transaction.go (lines 583-650: reactive completion)
- tmp/beads/cmd/bd/types.go (line 26: convoy description)
title: Add Convoy issue type support

description:
**Problem**
Go beads supports `bd relate <id1> <id2>` and `bd unrelate <id1> <id2>` for creating bidirectional \"see also\" links between issues. This enables knowledge graph connections without blocking or hierarchy. beads-rs needs the same capability.

**How it works in Go beads**
- Commands: `bd relate` and `bd unrelate` (in cmd/bd/relate.go)
- Creates bidirectional relates-to links using the dependency API
- Uses `DepRelatesTo` dependency type (stored in dependencies table)
- Both issues reference each other (id1 -> id2 and id2 -> id1)
- Non-blocking relationship (doesn't affect ready work calculation)

**Design for Rust**
1. Add `RelatesTo` variant to `DepKind` enum in src/core/domain.rs
2. Mark it as non-DAG-enforcing (like Related and DiscoveredFrom)
3. Add CLI commands: src/cli/commands/relate.rs
   - `bd relate <id1> <id2>` - creates both edges atomically
   - `bd unrelate <id1> <id2>` - removes both edges atomically
4. Wire through daemon IPC in src/daemon/ for atomic bidirectional operations
5. Add to CLI command tree in src/cli/mod.rs

**Design considerations**
- Bidirectional atomicity: both edges created/removed in same operation
- Prevent self-relations (id1 == id2)
- Handle partial failures gracefully
- JSON output shows both IDs and relation status

**Files to study**
- tmp/beads/cmd/bd/relate.go - Go implementation reference
- tmp/beads/internal/types/types.go - DepRelatesTo type definition
- src/core/domain.rs - where DepKind lives
- src/cli/commands/dep.rs - similar command pattern

**Acceptance**
- [ ] RelatesTo added to DepKind enum
- [ ] bd relate command creates bidirectional links
- [ ] bd unrelate command removes bidirectional links
- [ ] Self-relations rejected with clear error
- [ ] JSON output format matches Go beads
- [ ] Cargo fmt, clippy, test all pass
title: Add relate/unrelate CLI commands for bidirectional graph links

description:
**Problem**
After `beads-rs-fblt.15`, the daemon-owned deterministic harness covers restart/backlog/fresh-handshake semantics, but `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs` no longer has an external-process restart-under-tailnet/proxy proof. That leaves the combined daemon restart + IPC reconnect + proxy-process seam unproved at the product layer.

**Design**
Add back one thin external-process proof for the restart/offline/tailnet combination, or otherwise prove the same shipped seam without reintroducing the old heavyweight fault-injection cases. Keep it intentionally thin and clearly assembly-owned.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] The product seam still has one explicit restart-under-tailnet/proxy proof after the deterministic move
- [ ] The test stays thin enough to remain assembly-owned smoke, not a duplicate deep harness
- [ ] Verification proves the intended external restart/proxy seam

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/AGENTS.md`
title: Restore an external restart-under-tailnet product proof after moving deterministic REPL coverage
